Neonatal mortality defined as death of neonatal in time period Birth to 28 day after Birth.
\text{NMR} = \frac{\text{Total no of death of neonatal}}{\text{Total no of live Birth}} \times 1000
In India 61% of children death in first month after birth.
Neonatal mortality is important indicator of health services.
## Most Common Cause of Neonatal Mortality
## Neonatal mortality tells about Quality of Health Services.
In developed country neonatal mortality rate is very.
In developing neonatal mortality rate very high been poor education and low socio-economic status, poor health services.
If improves health services decreases the neonatal mortality rate.
Component of essential newborn care available in subcentre, public health care and home.
Important component of essential newborn care-
① Airway - Newborn child have clear airway. It's important part of care. If any obstruction available in airway can cause difficulty in Breathing. In sufficient Breathing cause Ganasis.

Most of death of neonatal (Newborn child due to hypothermia. Newborn baby unable to regulate the internal temperature. If Body temperature is unregulate can cause death of baby. Main precaution take avoid Bathing after birth. Baby covered with clothes after birth avoid heat loss.
Maximum heat loss of baby from head.
It is necessary to cover Baby head.
Birth of Baby → weight of Baby → Again baby covered uncovered covered and transfer to mother.
New born baby dermis is very soft and lack of dryness mechanism.
Minimum time to avoid Bath is 15 day.
Donot apply unnecessary things like creame, powder and lotion.
In new born baby not apply "Kayal" in eye.
These foreign substance cause irritation in eye.
Also cause toxic effect in eye.
Always eye clean inside to outside with clean clothes.

Cord care very important after birth: been most of injection occurs in cords. Donot apply any thing in cord which may be causes injection. Clean cord with the help of clean and sterile cloths. If any complication occurs in cord go to doctor.
|  Respiratory Rate | Absent | irregular & slow | Regular  |
|  Muscle tone | Placard | Some flexanion | Active motion  |
|  Rylex | Absent | Grimace | Ery, cough, snuze  |
|  Appearance | Bluish Body | Body Pink but
Breast feeding important part of newborn care.
Breast feeding provide nutrition and defense to baby.
|  1-3 day after birth | 3 day - 2 week | >2 week  |
Complete source of diet for baby
→ In normal vaginal delivery breast feeding start with 1 hour after birth.
→ In ceseration delivery breast feeding start with 4 hours after birth.
→ Exclusive breast feeding 6 month and expanded up to 2 years.
Baby friendly hospital initiates by UNICEF which promote Breast feeding.
# Mrs X agent 30 years delivered a female child, 3 month back. Currently she exclusively breast feed her child.
Most suitable family planning method for this couple is intra uterine device.
Intra uterine device most effective method of family planning.
IUD long lasting and very high protection against birth.
Intra uterine device very effective contraceptive method.
Intra uterine device have many advantage and side effect.
- Third generation IUD is Copper releasing
- IIIrd generation IUD is Hormonal releasing IUD
→ Very low failure rate of intra uterine device.
→ Intra uterine device can use large duration.
→ Intra uterine device is ideal contraceptive for Grap b/w two birth.
→ Intra uterine device protect against unwanted pregnancy.
→ It is available in subcentre and public health centre.
→ Intra uterine device very low side effect.
→ Adverse effect of IUD is minimum caparison to other contraceptive method.
→ IUD is very cheap method of contraception.
## National Family Planning Programme in India
First national programme launched in India for family planning in 1952.
This programme help to India in population exploitation management.
- Increase the use of family planning method
- Family planning easily available in subcarbie and public health centre.
- Reduce the prevalence of HIV and STI.
- Mission parivar Vikash programme help to people choose family planning method.
- Promote the Health education about family planning.
- These programme start for new married couple.
- Naji phel kit promote the carbaceptive method use.
- ASHA worker provide these kit in newly married couple.
- These kit contain condom, sanitary pad, OCP and other daily use thing.
- 500 g for first child birth after 2 year of marriage.
- 500 g for second child birth after 3 year of gap.
- 5 g for one packet oral carbaceptive pills.
Counselling of eligible couple for family planning is best method of approach.
- Help to couple to choose family planning method
- These approach help to couple choose most effective contraceptive method.
- Most effective approach to promote family planning method.
- Educate people about family planning method.
Abnormal population growth of country in short time interval is called population explosion.
Uncontrolled population growth of country is called population explosion.
Most of developing country suffers from population explosion.
In population explosion birth rate is high and death rate is lowest.
- Current trend of India is Late expanding because birth rate lowering and death rate also decline.
→ fertility rate of current India is decreases
## Measures of control of population explosion-
→ Control of population explosion best method is
start a programme who help in
→ Strengthen health care services to provide
→ promote the sex education and provide
→ Decrease the MRP of contraceptive method.
→ These measures help to control population
Social security define as method who decrease the social risk like - unemployment, impairment, medical emergency, sickness, dependent, maternity etc.
ESI act formed in 1948. This act help employees to cover social security.
- All industry are eligible except for Railway Army, mine.
- Factory with minimum 10 workers with electricity is mandatory for ESI.
- Factory with minimum 20 workers without use of electricity is mandatory for ESI.
→ Primary health care first link b/w individuals, family and community to public health services.
→ Primary health care provided basic health care.
→ Primary health care promote health education and nutrition support.
→ Primary health care help vaccination, prevention and control of diseases.
→ Primary health care have Subcentre, Public Health centre and aganandii centre.
→ Subcentre present 3000-5000 population and run by ANM and MPW.
→ Public Health centre present 30000-30000 population and run by Medical offices and Nursing offices, paramedical staff.
# Public Health Care in Rural Area
- Rural area are mostly remote area of India.
- These are suffered most of communication disease and nutrition, malnutrition, and other.
- At village level Aganwadi centre present.
- Aganwadi centre run by Aganwadi workers.
- These centre provide nutritional support, Immunization, Health education, ORS, Vit A supplement.
- These centre record the health status of village.
- Subcentre at village level run by ANM and MPW.
- Subcentre provide Antinatal care, Perinatal care and safe delivery.
- These subcentre provide family planning method and essential medicine.
Any waste generated during diagnosis, treatment, vaccination of human and animal and biomedical research.
These waste are classified in color coding system.
C → Soiled, cotton Bendge, plaster cast etc.
D → Disposed medicine, experienced medicine cyto-toxic drug
F → "X-ray" films fluid, Aispivated Body fluid
Urine bag, Foley's catholic, vacutainer with out blood, Inguison set, gloves etc.
# Biomedical waste disposal according as per National and State level-
Blue - Bin made of Card board.
Injections waste required pre treatment before disposal.
Unwanted event leading to - Human & Health Damage
- Things collect and shift in high places.
- obey and follow instruction of NORF.
- Anaemia nukt Bharat programme provide prophylaxis of Anaemia
- ASMA, Agom wadi worker provide the Iron and folic acid supplement
- Pregnant female required 60 mg Iron and 500 mcg of folic acid
- These prophylaxis provided free of cast.
- Red cross founded by Dr. Henry Dropuller.
- Headquarter of Red cross in Geneva (Switzerland).
- In India Headquarter of Red cross in Bangalore (Karnataka).
- Red cross provided Health care service during the war.
- Red cross also provide ambulance, Health facilities during disaster.
- Red cross organization available in all over the world.
- Red cross society always available in war situation.
## Analysis the health situation - This is first step of planning cycle.
→ These step provide data which help the assess the health situation.
→ Deep analysis of Health situation help to establishment of Object & Goal.
- National mental health programme provided mental health related education and treatment.
- These programmes provided all district mental health.
- These programmes reach people who have mental disorders, Drug Abuse.
- These programmes provided mental health related education.
1. Promote health education especially mental health education.
2. Treatment provided to mental illness people.
→ Drug substance used by individual which modified one or more than one function is called Drug Abuse.
→ Drug dependence — Cocaine, Alcohol, Chases white powder, Diazepam etc.
→ These drug use and drug dependence drug.
→ Drug Independence — Steroids are Drug Independence drug.
